Rania Khalil is a Python Developer with experience in computer vision for the banking domain, proficient in OpenCV, PIL, and Numpy, and skilled in unit testing, debugging, and Git version control. Her contact information includes her email, phone number, GitHub, and LinkedIn profiles.
Rania Khalil obtained a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science from Savitribai Phule Pune University in June 2018.

From July 2018 to the present, Rania Khalil has worked as a Python Developer at Atos Syntel in Cairo, Egypt, where she designed and developed computer vision algorithms for a US-based client in the banking domain, performed unit testing and debugging, and maintained code versions using Git.
In 2017, Rania Khalil developed and presented a poster on a 'Smart Ambulance' system, winning first place in a college-level competition for innovation and presentation clarity.

Rania Khalil completed several certificates in 2015, 2016, and 2017, including the ESD Program at Zensar Technologies, Employability Training at Barclays, and workshops on Medical IOT and Introduction to Arduino.
Rania Khalil received awards and honors in 2017 and 2018, including first rank for her poster presentation, project idea selection for prototyping, and sponsorship from Platinum Auto.
Rania Khalil lists her skills as Python programming, Django, OpenCV, PIL, Numpy, HTML5, REST API, MySQL, Git, Version Control, Unit Testing, and Debugging.
Rania Khalil served as Treasurer of the IEEE Student Branch from 2017 to 2018 at JSCOE, Pune, managing finances and organizing an IEEE workshop on 'Excellence in English and Public Speaking'.
From 2016 to 2017, Rania Khalil was a Team Leader at JSCOE, Pune, leading teams in organizing events and participating in technical competitions.

Rania Khalil is a driven Python Developer currently building computer vision solutions for the banking sector at Atos Syntel in Cairo. A 2018 Computer Science graduate of Savitribai Phule Pune University, she quickly translated academic promise into practical expertise. Her toolkit is robust – Python, Django, OpenCV, PIL, and NumPy form the core, complemented by skills in HTML5, REST APIs, and MySQL. She’s meticulous about code quality, proficient in unit testing, debugging, and leveraging Git for version control. 

But Rania’s ambition extends beyond proficient coding. Even during her studies at JSCOE, Pune, she demonstrated a knack for innovation, winning first place for a ‘Smart Ambulance’ system poster presentation in 2017 – a project recognized not only for its ingenuity but also for its clear communication. This inventive spirit earned her project idea selection for prototyping and sponsorship from Platinum Auto. 

Leadership also comes naturally to Rania. She honed her organizational skills as Treasurer of the IEEE Student Branch, managing finances and orchestrating workshops, and previously as a Team Leader, guiding event organization and technical competition participation. Early career development programs at Zensar Technologies and Barclays, alongside workshops in Medical IoT and Arduino, laid a strong foundation, revealing a continuous learner eager to expand her skillset. She’s a pragmatic problem-solver, a collaborative team player, and a rising talent in the world of computer vision.

2017 was a year of burgeoning recognition for Rania Khalil. It marked the peak of projects begun in previous years of focused skill-building – certificates earned in 2015 and 2016 from Zensar Technologies, Barclays, and introductory workshops in Medical IoT and Arduino all coalesced. This foundation propelled her to first place in a college-level competition with a strikingly innovative ‘Smart Ambulance’ system, a poster presentation lauded for both its ingenuity and clarity. The success didn’t end there; her project idea was selected for prototyping, attracting sponsorship from Platinum Auto, and cementing her reputation as a rising talent. Beyond individual achievement, 2017 saw Rania step into a leadership role as Treasurer of the IEEE Student Branch at JSCOE, Pune. She wasn’t merely a custodian of funds, but a proactive organizer, successfully hosting an IEEE workshop focused on honing crucial soft skills – Excellence in English and Public Speaking. This year wasn’t a culmination, but a powerful springboard, building on her experience as a Team Leader from the previous year and foreshadowing further accolades in 2018. It was a year where technical prowess met organizational ability, and a promising student truly began to shine.

Rania Khalil is a dedicated Python Developer, a builder of sight for the financial world. For over five years, since July 2018, she’s honed her craft at Atos Syntel in Cairo, Egypt, crafting computer vision algorithms for a US banking client. She doesn’t just write code; she *gives* systems the ability to see, to analyze, and to understand images – a crucial skill in a world increasingly reliant on visual data. 

Her toolkit is precise and powerful. OpenCV, PIL, and Numpy are extensions of her own thinking, allowing her to manipulate and interpret images with finesse. But technical skill is only part of the story. Rania understands the importance of a solid foundation, meticulously crafting unit tests and relentlessly debugging to ensure her creations are robust and reliable. She’s a guardian of code integrity, expertly managing versions with Git, ensuring a clear history and collaborative workflow. 

She’s a problem-solver, a detail-oriented architect of digital perception, and a professional readily connectable through her email, phone, GitHub, and LinkedIn profiles. Rania isn’t simply a coder; she’s a visual intelligence specialist, shaping the future of banking, one algorithm at a time.
